reviews.review_title: Still have to listen to jerks talking
reviews.review_description:
I connected to the Muse show okay. Could look higher def, I suppose. But you included the one thing I hate about going to shows, standing next to and behind other people and having to listen to them chat louder than the performance. Why was this necessary to do?

Gave it a second chance with the preview this morning. Someone's avatar was standing directly on top of me! Boy, I thought real people were annoying, but having pieces of an avatar phasing in and out in my line of vision as they move makes me long for the days of a 6-foot guy standing in front of me. WHY is this even possible???? I'm out!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback!  The 360 stereo video requires a good internet connection for the best results, 30Mbps for Stereo 4k. If lower you will see degraded tiles or it will switch to mono and you will lose depth.
Regarding the voices, if you click the Audio settings on your wrist menu, you can turn Voices off completely, and if you move yourself to the front of the virtual viewpoint or away from the other avatars they fade away. Hope this helps!

reviews.review_title: Low quality video & it's only in English
reviews.review_description:
The main claim of the application is to view 3D videos (concerts, theater etc). If the video is of low quality, it makes no sense to install this application, let alone pay for the content.

In a globalized world, localizing texts into a single language is shooting yourself in the foot. The application should be translated into at least the 5 or 6 most spoken languages in the world. Spanish, English, Chinese ...

*****************************
Response to the developer
*****************************

The problem is not the speed of the connection. My connection is 1000 Mbps, and the requirements are only 30 Mbps.

The problem is that a 4K video at 360 degrees, is too low resolution video to play in VR. I have seen 8K and 12K video at 180 degrees, and the image starts to look decent at close proximity. At this point you can't promote videos with only 4K.

I don't think you have ever seen a VR video at 8K or 12K with 180 degrees. Then you will see the difference.
After the last update problems are gone, what an awesome experience ❤️
Won't let me sign in no keyboard

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you have had problems, we have released an updated version today and invite you to download it and see if it resolves your problem. If not please contact support@stageverse.com and we will investigate!

reviews.review_description: Requested a confirmation code multiple times - to both email and phone - and it none of them worked. Oh well, won’t be trying out muse content…

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear you have had problems, we had some teething problems on day 1. We have released an updated version addressing some of these problems and invite you to try it out. FYI We are serving 4K 360 Stereoscopic video, and as such its best viewed with at least a 30Mbps connection. It will switch to monoscopic if the connection is not fast enough and then degrade further to a low quality stream after that.
Thank you

reviews.review_title: I felt like i was in the concert again
reviews.review_description: Excellent app, this must be at the official appstore

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words, much appreciated!

reviews.review_description:
Just watched the demo content, and the effect is pretty well realized with the 360 degree 3D camera rigs. Not as much depth or clarity as you'd hope for, but still better than the typical flat 360 bubbles that have been the norm for these virtual events in the past. You can spot the 360 degree camera rig from some of the viewpoints, and I'd almost like to see a tech "Behind the scenes" into how this all comes together. First show starts in a half hour for free. Can't beat that. Not sure what it would be worth if not free, but interesting none the less.

UPDATE: Watched the first Muse show. Very cool. It did take a few retries to get in, and it looked like there were over 1,000 people connecting at the very start, so I imagine it's growing pains. Once I was in, it was pretty great. You are in a room with 10 or so others. Would be very fun to get a group of friends and join in together. I highly recommend going to the Map and choosing the "Follow the Action" option. They have a lot more camera angles than the few static spots they let you choose around the stage and it edits between them really well. A really cool experience. After each song it would force me to lock into one of the static cameras again and I would have to switch back to the "Follow the Action" auto view cam, but otherwise a really cool way to experience it. Definitely check it out for this Muse show. It's a lot of fun, for free, and a lot better than previous attempts at 360 degree flat static camera concerts in my experience.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words and constructive feedback, it's much appreciated. We did indeed have some teething problems on day 1 but we've addressed a lot of those now. We are glad you enjoyed the show!  The video content is being served at 4K 360 Stereo, so best viewed on 30Mbps or faster connection otherwise it will degrade to Monoscopic and then to a low quality stream. We're working to introduce a better adaptive bitrate solution soon.

reviews.review_description: Some kinda music show social platform. Doesn't matter. I liked the hub environment, and that's what I judge these fly-by-night VR metaspheres.
Incredible and I supose this is only the begining! Please, the show must be continue!! ;)

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind feedback :)

reviews.review_description:
Lags so bad with streaming and can only get sound for around 5 seconds before it cuts out and I have to switch viewpoints to get another 5 seconds of sound.

Please allow downloading of the concert (Muse) to view offline in 4K - would happily pay full price
Very nice experience, only lack the ability to share via photo, video or selfie

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words! We have enabled the ability to share now!

reviews.review_description: Watched the incredible Muse concert all the way through last night/this morning. Fantastic experience. As others have pointed out though could've done without everyone there being added to the party when you come and go, as you get constant notifications when it happens. Aside from that it was superb, and the most time I have spent in VR in one sitting. Highly recommended.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words!  We are glad you enjoyed the experience. We've noted your feedback about the notifications and it will be addressed soon.

reviews.review_description:
I love the ease of use and the sound quality / image quality is fine for me. The main issues I have so far are 
1) instantly being added to a 'party' of strangers
2) having people jump in and out of the 'party' 20 times a second and be notified each time they do with a huge popup notification
I have used quite a few apps for virtual events and this one has promise, but you seriously need to do less to break immersion constantly.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback, we appreciate it! 
1. You can create a private party from the menu system, open the menu, click on Stageverse Stadium and click Start Party, make it "Private" you can then share the private code with your friends and no one else can join.
2. Thank you, we are addressing this in the next release!

reviews.review_description: I attempted to watch the muse concert. Key word: attempted. Aside from it crashing ever 3 mins and long load times making me feel like Im using a 10 year old hard drive. The audio would cut out every two seconds. I'm not trying again

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear you have had problems, we had some teething problems on day1 and we have also released an updated version addressing some of these. We invite you to download it and see if you are still having the same issue. FYI We are serving 4K 360 Stereoscopic video, and as such its best viewed with at least a 30Mbps connection. It will switch to monoscopic if the connection is not fast enough and then degrade further to a low quality stream after that.
Thank you

reviews.review_description:
I want to preface this with the fact that I am aware the issues are most likely caused or exaggarated by my relatively slow (16 Mbit/s) internet connection.

I really like the idea and wanted it to be a great experience, but it was unenjoybale and nauseating. The video quality jumps from potato to slightly higher resolution potato but will mostly hover around the 240p quality. The video has no depth, it remains a flat 360° video experience, very rarely would the video turn into a proper stereoscopic image for about a second before it returned to flat.

The constant shifting in video quality was nauseating, and when I was watching a viewpoint with a non-stationary camera, the issue was even worse, unfortunately I had to quit after a few minutes of watching.

There desperately needs to be an option to pre-buffer large portions of the video feed so people with a slower internet connection are not left in the dust.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback, what you are experiencing is purely due to the Internet connection not being fast enough. The video content is 4K 360 stereoscopic video, you will need at least 30Mbps to fully enjoy the content otherwise as you mention it degrades to mono and then further to very low quality. We are working to improve this but please try again on a faster connection and thank you for your feedback.

reviews.review_description:
I don’t get the bad reviews. This is new, it cannot be flawless. The definition is not the same as what you would get with a blu-ray using a HD screen, I see a lot of people complaining about that, but in fact it could not be better, especially when it is in 3D - as it is, the higher quality I got was enough for me to enjoy every single moment of the show and get hypnotised, it was magical. You just have to be aware of the limitations of the technology itself.
Of course, as I have an average connection, I did experience some quality variations, sudden losses, or a few freezes - but even all of this ran smoothly, nothing more than half a second. So yes, the image sometimes switched from 3D to 2D, or got blurry for a little while. But I could feel everything had been thought to give me the best experience ever - and this is what I got. I did not even really know Muse before, and I just wanted to try the app to see what a show could become if it was aired on VR. And it is outstanding. It gave me the chills, I felt like I could see everything better than if I had been there. Do not get me wrong, I am not saying VR should ever replace real shows, but it is such a nice way to share it with people who could not be there. I could watch this again and again - I think I will, actually. And I could pay the price of a ticket for this, honestly. Remember that it is just the early stage of VR technology, so there are limitations, but when you see what has been done here already… Wow ! It is definitely a good start. 
I hope one day the tech will allow to download the whole video at once so that it runs even better - but I get that right now it would be impossible as the size of the file would take all the space of the headset memory. 
The only thing I would like to change in order to get the perfect experience would be an option to deactivate completely the social feature. I think the social feature is part of the experience, but one should be able to watch the show without it. I know you can mute the audio of other people, I know you can move to the edge of the watching area to avoid seing them, but it is not enough - especially because you still get notifications when they join or leave the party. The social feature is good when you want to chat, maybe for sports events or for shows you have already seen - but then you should decide whenever you want to use it, maybe you could include a dedicated area in each space, and keep it activated for all users only when they are in the lobby, far from the stage ? 

I really, really enjoyed the "follow the action" feature that allows you to watch the show automatically from selected viewpoints, with great transitions. The only thing is, you have to reactivate it each time a new song starts - maybe this could be improved too.
And I think the fact you can only see the show at certain dedicated time slots makes the experience even more thrilling, it feels like you have to get prepared and wait as if it was live.

Thank you so much for creating this beautiful, incredible and moving experience. It gives so much hope regarding the possible cultural usages of VR. You did an amazing work.

reviews.review_title: Exactly what I want in a VR concert
reviews.review_description: I'm so glad this app is finally becoming available.  I've wanted a real VR concert experience for a while.  I will be happy to pay a reasonable price to experience live concerts this way. Yes, the video isn't 4k quality, but it is in a dark environment and is limited to bandwidth so that is understandable. I don't understand the complaints about people talking over the show because you can mute anyone in the app. Also, there are plenty of places to move around so no problem sharing the event with others. I do wish you could get rid of the constant notices about people coming and going from the party. Also, eventually it would be awesome to have 3d sound as well.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, we appreciate it! FYI the video content is being served at 4K 360 Stereoscopic and to get the full quality stream you need around 30Mbps or faster connection. The experience will degrade to monoscopic when it cannot keep up and then further degraded if it is unable to display monoscopic. Your feedback regarding the notifications has been taken onboard.

reviews.review_description: I installed it on Quest 2 but it keeps saying “update required” everytime I launch it. I’ve rebooted the headset, updated the OS on the headset, updated the Oculus app on my iPhone but nothing will make the app work.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you have had problems, we have released an updated version and invite you to download it and see if you are still having the same issue. Thank you!

reviews.review_description: Stageverse i believe in you. I hope that will be one of Top 10 vr platform. When will be everythik done ? 🫡👌❤️

reviews.review_title: Free but still feel like Igot ripped off
reviews.review_description:
I understand this is free content, and so you're probably trading your data for it, but to be forced through that sign up process just to see absolute potato quality video streams and unnatural/headache-inducing viewpoints of the concert just seems like a scam.  You'll have a much better experience watching a 2D stream of a concert on YouTube or something.

On the plus side, at least there aren't any screaming kids. Yet.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback, it sounds as though your internet connection was unable to stream the content at its original quality. The video is 4K 360 Stereoscopic and for best results you need at least 30Mbps connection.  The player will switch to mono if it is unable to stream the higher quality and then further reduce quality if it is unable to meet that either.
Please try again on a fast connection and let us know what you think about the video quality.
